private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
Reflex Aerospace is a Series A-stage space and aerospace company that has raised €50M+. The valuation analysis relies on a comparable company methodology across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers: Aalyria ($1.3B, Series B), ClearSpace ($400M, Series A), Hubble Network ($400M, Series B), and Sift ($274M, Series B).

Peer-median triangulation yields a $400M implied valuation, anchored to the median of the four-company comp set. The peer range spans $274M to $1.3B, reflecting stage and maturity variance within the cohort. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 53.2x across five public tickers: RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) could not be applied, as Reflex Aerospace's revenue is not on file—a common constraint for private-stage companies.

The triangulated valuation range is $340M to $460M, with a $400M median. This analysis is supported by a single applicable method, making stage and business model alignment with peer comparables the primary anchor. Key uncertainties include the lack of disclosed revenue, which precludes revenue-multiple cross-checks, and the limited applicability of the public comp set to early-stage private dynamics. The wide peer range ($274M–$1.3B) underscores material variation in how the market values space/aerospace businesses at this stage, suggesting stage advancement and specific operational milestones could meaningfully shift valuation within this band.
